test_case: Capture from BlackHole 2ch for 60 seconds and measure sample-continuity
reason_not_measured: Arbiter host is Windows 11; no macOS Apple-Silicon hardware available at spike time. The acceptance matrix entry for issue #450 explicitly authorises this path: 'Requires real macOS hardware to satisfy measurement test cases; if unavailable, evidence must explicitly record the limitation and emit a follow-up spike issue.'

measurement_protocol.host_requirement: Apple Silicon Mac running macOS 13.x or 14.x
preconditions: System Settings → Sound → Output set to 'BlackHole 2ch' (or a Multi-Output Device that includes it)
Terminal that will host the process has Microphone permission granted in Privacy & Security
Audio source playing for the full 60 s (e.g., a known sine sweep or the project's audio harness fixture)
measurement_protocol.harness: verification-evidence/macos/macos-01-screencapturekit-prototype.md §4 (Tier-2 cpal sketch). Wire it into a small bin and run for exactly 60 s.
steps: Start the harness; record monotonic clock t0 at first audio callback
Accumulate i16 samples until 60.000 s elapsed
Detect discontinuities by emitting a known sine input and checking phase continuity across chunk boundaries
Record dropped_chunks from the cpal error callback counter
Write the populated JSON back to this file path

notes: This file intentionally records 'not_measured'. Populating 'result' requires the MACOS-01b follow-up spike on real hardware. Do NOT fabricate numbers.
